ranked_movies.title: Airplane!
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a pinnacle of comedy filmmaking and a classic example of the genre from the discussions about David Zucker's work and the art of comedy.

ranked_movies.title: The Naked Gun
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended and discussed as a modern comedy success and a benchmark for the genre, especially in the context of its recent reboot and David Zucker's legacy.

ranked_movies.title: Shaun of the Dead
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently brought up as a top-tier horror-comedy and a prime example of successful British comedy filmmaking.

ranked_movies.title: What We Do in the Shadows
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a modern classic in the horror-comedy genre and a go-to recommendation for a fun movie night.

ranked_movies.title: Tucker and Dale vs Evil
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended as a hilarious and clever horror-comedy that subverts genre tropes.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed not as a comedy, but as a prime example of a horror film whose performances (Toni Collette) were unjustly overlooked by the Oscars, highlighting the bias against genre films.

ranked_movies.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as an example of a comedic actor (Jim Carrey) delivering an acclaimed dramatic performance that was still overlooked by awards shows.

ranked_movies.title: Mrs. Doubtfire
ranked_movies.reason: Listed among the top family-friendly comedies from the 80s/90s that are still beloved and recommended for all ages.

ranked_movies.title: Home Alone
ranked_movies.reason: A perennial favorite and highly recommended family comedy from the requested era.

ranked_movies.title: The Big Short
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as an example of a director (Adam McKay) successfully transitioning from broad comedy to acclaimed dramatic filmmaking.

ranked_movies.title: Bram Stoker's Dracula
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively debated for its tonal shifts and its approach as an adaptation, with many finding its over-the-top style unintentionally comedic.

ranked_movies.title: Barry
ranked_movies.reason: The TV show was highlighted as an example of a comedic actor (Bill Hader) delivering an astounding dramatic performance in a genre-blending series.

ranked_movies.title: My Cousin Vinny
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced as one of the few genuine comedies to win a major Oscar (Marisa Tomei for Best Supporting Actress).

ranked_movies.title: Little Miss Sunshine
ranked_movies.reason: Noted as a comedy that received significant Oscar recognition, including a win for Alan Arkin.

ranked_movies.title: The Truman Show
ranked_movies.reason: Another example of Jim Carrey's acclaimed dramatic work that was overlooked by the Academy during awards season.

ranked_movies.title: Withnail and I
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a cult classic offbeat British comedy with a dedicated following.

ranked_movies.title: Idle Hands
ranked_movies.reason: A frequently suggested fun horror-comedy for a movie night.

ranked_movies.title: Kindergarten Cop
ranked_movies.reason: Listed among the recommended family comedies from the 90s that are still popular today.

ranked_movies.title: Zombieland
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a successful and fun modern horror-comedy.

ranked_movies.title: A Fish Called Wanda
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a classic example of British comedy filmmaking.
